sub_steps.title: Sub-Steps (Temporal Divisions)
sub_steps.hover: Dividing the frame time delta into smaller steps to prevent high-velocity objects from clipping/tunnelling through boundaries.

iterations.title: Iterations (Gauss-Seidel Loops)
iterations.hover: The number of solver loop passes executed per sub-step, ensuring connected joints converge to a rigid, steel-like behavior.

collision.title: Collision (Interaction)
collision.hover: Physical contact between body colliders, triggering normal bounce impulses to resolve intersections.

penetration.title: Collision Penetration (Clipping)
penetration.hover: Intersecting or clipping between colliders due to discrete steps, resolved by rewriting coordinates via Baumgarte correction.

friction.title: Friction (Lateral Resistance)
friction.hover: Resistive force acting tangentially to oppose sliding motion, capped via Coulomb's law (ft <= mu * fn).

joints.title: Articulated Joints (Linkages)
joints.hover: Restricting relative translation and rotation parameters (DOF) between rigid bodies using strict constraint rules.

x_i.title: Target State x_i
x_i.hover: The current converged value of the active constraint coordinate (velocity or position correction).
x_i.deep_dive: The target state variable x_i represents the final converged position or velocity of a dynamic body. Inside C++ engines, the accumulated impulses are dynamically updated and clamped in RAM across solver loops to prevent violent coordinate explosions.

a_ii.title: Diagonal Element a_ii
a_ii.hover: The matrix diagonal representation of active constraint effective mass or rotational inertia.
a_ii.deep_dive: The diagonal matrix element a_ii represents the active body's effective mass or rotational inertia. It is computed in C++ engines using coordinate-specific cross-product matrices. Its inverse (1/a_ii) is precalculated to avoid slow division math at the silicon level.

b_i.title: Bias Element b_i
b_i.hover: Accumulated external forces (gravity, compliance terms, and penetration error parameters).
b_i.deep_dive: The bias vector element b_i represents raw external forces (gravity) or positional errors like joint stretch separation. The engine measures this visual error, multiplies it by a stiffness coefficient, and pushes the solver to pull the bodies back into place.

sum_t.title: Resolved Summation sum_t
sum_t.hover: Cumulative force feedback from neighboring constraints already resolved in the current sweep (present state k+1).
sum_t.deep_dive: The resolved summation represents feedback forces from constraints sitting earlier in the sweep sequence (such as the top anchors T-array). Their math is already solved for this frame, allowing the CPU to read linear velocities directly from L1 cache in a warm-started state.

sum_b.title: Unresolved Summation sum_b
sum_b.hover: Cumulative force feedback from neighboring constraints pending calculations in the current loop (historical state k).
sum_b.deep_dive: The unresolved summation represents feedback forces from connected constraints sitting later in the execution sweep (such as the bottom hanging array). The engine recycles historical data from the previous sub-step pass to compute their temporary influence.

a_ij.title: Coupling Coefficient a_ij
a_ij.hover: Force transmission transmission efficiency and stiffness mapping between adjacent constraint coordinates.
a_ij.deep_dive: The coupling coefficient mapping defines the interaction stiffness and damping parameters between adjacent bodies. It dictates force transmission efficiency across articulation registers config parameters in Unity/Unreal inspectors.

temporal.title: Temporal Coherence (Time History)
temporal.hover: Recycling final constraint forces from the previous frame as the warm-starting initial guess for the current step.

mobility.title: Mobility (Inverse Inertia)
mobility.hover: The mathematical reciprocal of mass (1/mass) used to replace division latency with fast multipliers.

unbalanced.title: Unbalanced Force (Residual)
unbalanced.hover: Residual mathematical error representing the net force imbalance acting on a body before convergence.

slip.title: Slip (Correction Displacement)
slip.hover: The specific coordinate shift applied to bodies to eliminate force residual imbalances.

static.title: BodyType::Static
static.hover: Immovable anchors representing infinite physical mass values in code.

inv_mass.title: Inverse Mass (1/M)
inv_mass.hover: Inverted mass value hardcoded to 0.0 to bypass branch prediction condition checks inside CPU execution lanes.
